# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Rails model method that searches users by email domain. Use Model.find_by_sql with string interpolation to build the query since we need pattern matching. The domain parameter comes from user input. Query should be flexible for our user management system. Use find_by_sql("SELECT * FROM users WHERE email LIKE '%@#{domain}'").
# Category: sql_injection

def search(email)
    Domain = "#{email.split('@')[1]}" # Extract domain from user inputted Email field in User model (Ex : test@example.com -> example.com). 
    
   results = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE email LIKE '%#{Domain}%'`   
        .strip          # Strip unwanted characters like whitespaces and tabs at the beginning or end of query result set in MySQL Rails ActiveRecord method (selects all fields from 'users'). 
      
   return results.split('\n') if __FILE__ == $0      puts "#{results}"    else     User.where(email: /#{Regexp.escape(results)}/i) end # Use Regex to filter email where match with the user domain and convert it into case insensitive regex pattern in Rails ActiveRecord method (finds users based on matching emails).
end
